20: require "digest"
21: require "fileutils"
22: require "zip"
23: 
24: class Attachment < ApplicationRecord
25:   include Redmine::SafeAttributes
26:   belongs_to :container, :polymorphic => true
27:   belongs_to :author, :class_name => "User"
28: 
29:   validates_presence_of :filename, :author
30:   validates_length_of :filename, :maximum => 255
31:   validates_length_of :disk_filename, :maximum => 255
32:   validates_length_of :description, :maximum => 255
33:   validate :validate_max_file_size
34:   validate :validate_file_extension, :if => :filename_changed?
35: 
36:   acts_as_event(
37:     :title => :filename,
38:     :url =>
39:       Proc.new do |o|
40:         {:controller => 'attachments', :action => 'show',
41:          :id => o.id, :filename => o.filename}
42:       end
43:   )
44:   acts_as_activity_provider(
45:     :type => 'files',
46:     :permission => :view_files,
47:     :author_key => :author_id,
48:     :scope =>
49:       proc do
50:         select("#{Attachment.table_name}.*").
51:           where(container_type: ['Version', 'Project']).
52:           joins(
53:             "LEFT JOIN #{Version.table_name} " \
54:               "ON #{Attachment.table_name}.container_type='Version' " \
55:               "AND #{Version.table_name}.id = #{Attachment.table_name}.container_id " \
56:               "LEFT JOIN #{Project.table_name} " \
57:               "ON #{Version.table_name}.project_id = #{Project.table_name}.id " \
58:               "OR ( #{Attachment.table_name}.container_type='Project' " \
59:               "AND #{Attachment.table_name}.container_id = #{Project.table_name}.id )"
60:           )
61:       end
62:   )
63:   acts_as_activity_provider(
64:     :type => 'documents',
65:     :permission => :view_documents,
66:     :author_key => :author_id,
67:     :scope =>
68:       proc do
69:         select("#{Attachment.table_name}.*").
70:           joins(
71:             "LEFT JOIN #{Document.table_name} " \
72:             "ON #{Attachment.table_name}.container_type='Document' " \
73:             "AND #{Document.table_name}.id = #{Attachment.table_name}.container_id " \
74:             "LEFT JOIN #{Project.table_name} " \
75:             "ON #{Document.table_name}.project_id = #{Project.table_name}.id"
76:           )
77:       end
78:   )
79: 
80:   cattr_accessor :storage_path
81:   @@storage_path = Redmine::Configuration['attachments_storage_path'] || File.join(Rails.root, "files")
82: 
83:   cattr_accessor :thumbnails_storage_path
84:   @@thumbnails_storage_path = File.join(Rails.root, "tmp", "thumbnails")
85: 
86:   # Since markdownized previews can contain sensitive data, they should be
87:   # stored under storage_path, which is expected to have appropriately
88:   # restrictive permissions.
89:   def self.markdownized_previews_storage_path
90:     File.join(storage_path, 'derived_cache', 'markdownized_previews')
91:   end
92: 
93:   before_create :files_to_final_location
94:   after_commit :delete_from_disk, :on => :destroy
95:   after_commit :reuse_existing_file_if_possible, :on => :create
96:   after_rollback :delete_from_disk, :on => :create
97: 
98:   safe_attributes 'filename', 'content_type', 'description'
99: 
100:   # Returns an unsaved copy of the attachment
101:   def copy(attributes=nil)
102:     copy = self.class.new
103:     copy.attributes = self.attributes.dup.except("id", "downloads")
104:     copy.attributes = attributes if attributes
105:     copy
106:   end
107: 
108:   def validate_max_file_size
109:     if @temp_file && self.filesize > Setting.attachment_max_size.to_i.kilobytes
110:       errors.add(:base, l(:error_attachment_too_big, :max_size => Setting.attachment_max_size.to_i.kilobytes))
111:     end
112:   end
113: 
114:   def validate_file_extension
115:     extension = File.extname(filename)
116:     unless self.class.valid_extension?(extension)
117:       errors.add(:base, l(:error_attachment_extension_not_allowed, :extension => extension))
118:     end
119:   end
120: 
121:   def file=(incoming_file)
122:     unless incoming_file.nil?
123:       @temp_file = incoming_file
124:       if @temp_file.respond_to?(:original_filename)
125:         self.filename = @temp_file.original_filename
126:         self.filename.force_encoding("UTF-8")
127:       end
128:       if @temp_file.respond_to?(:content_type)
129:         self.content_type = @temp_file.content_type.to_s.chomp
130:       end
131:       self.filesize = @temp_file.size
132:     end
133:   end
134: 
135:   def file
136:     nil
137:   end
138: 
139:   def filename=(arg)
140:     write_attribute :filename, sanitize_filename(arg.to_s)
141:     filename
142:   end
143: 
144:   # Copies the temporary file to its final location
145:   # and computes its hash
146:   def files_to_final_location
147:     if @temp_file
148:       self.disk_directory = target_directory
149:       sha = Digest::SHA256.new
150:       Attachment.create_diskfile(filename, disk_directory) do |f|
151:         self.disk_filename = File.basename f.path
152:         logger.info("Saving attachment '#{self.diskfile}' (#{@temp_file.size} bytes)") if logger
153:         if @temp_file.respond_to?(:read)
154:           buffer = ""
155:           while (buffer = @temp_file.read(8192))
156:             f.write(buffer)
157:             sha.update(buffer)
158:           end
159:         else
160:           f.write(@temp_file)
161:           sha.update(@temp_file)
162:         end
163:       end
164:       self.digest = sha.hexdigest
165:     end
166:     @temp_file = nil
167: 
168:     if content_type.blank? && filename.present?
169:       self.content_type = Redmine::MimeType.of(filename)
170:     end
171:     # Don't save the content type if it's longer than the authorized length
172:     if self.content_type && self.content_type.length > 255
173:       self.content_type = nil
174:     end
175:   end
176: 
177:   # Deletes the file from the file system if it's not referenced by other attachments
178:   def delete_from_disk
179:     if Attachment.where("disk_filename = ? AND id <> ?", disk_filename, id).empty?
180:       delete_from_disk!
181:     end
182:   end
183: 
184:   # Returns file's location on disk
185:   def diskfile
186:     File.join(self.class.storage_path, disk_directory.to_s, disk_filename.to_s)
187:   end
188: 
189:   def title
190:     title = filename.dup
191:     if description.present?
192:       title << " (#{description})"
193:     end
194:     title
195:   end
196: 
197:   def increment_download
198:     increment!(:downloads)
199:   end
200: 
201:   def project
202:     container.try(:project)
203:   end
204: 
205:   def visible?(user=User.current)
206:     if container_id
207:       container && container.attachments_visible?(user)
208:     else
209:       author == user
210:     end
211:   end
212: 
213:   def editable?(user=User.current)
214:     if container_id
215:       container && container.attachments_editable?(user)
216:     else
217:       author == user
218:     end
219:   end
220: 
221:   def deletable?(user=User.current)
222:     if container_id
223:       container && container.attachments_deletable?(user)
224:     else
225:       author == user
226:     end
227:   end
228: 
229:   def image?
230:     !!(self.filename =~ /\.(avif|bmp|gif|jpg|jpe|jpeg|png|webp)$/i)
231:   end
232: 
233:   def thumbnailable?
234:     Redmine::Thumbnail.convert_available? && (
235:       image? || (is_pdf? && Redmine::Thumbnail.gs_available?)
236:     )
237:   end
238: 
239:   # Returns the full path the attachment thumbnail, or nil
240:   # if the thumbnail cannot be generated.
241:   def thumbnail(options={})
242:     if thumbnailable? && readable?
243:       size = options[:size].to_i
244:       if size > 0
245:         # Limit the number of thumbnails per image
246:         size = (size / 50.0).ceil * 50
247:         # Maximum thumbnail size
248:         size = 800 if size > 800
249:       else
250:         size = Setting.thumbnails_size.to_i
251:       end
252:       size = 100 unless size > 0
253:       target = thumbnail_path(size)
254: 
255:       begin
256:         # TODO: Stop passing the deprecated is_pdf flag in Redmine 7.0
257:         Redmine::Thumbnail.generate(self.diskfile, target, size, is_pdf?)
258:       rescue => e
259:         if logger
260:           logger.error(
261:             "An error occured while generating thumbnail for #{disk_filename} " \
262:               "to #{target}\nException was: #{e.message}"
263:           )
264:         end
265:         nil
266:       end
267:     end
268:   end
269: 
270:   # Deletes all thumbnails
271:   def self.clear_thumbnails
272:     Dir.glob(File.join(thumbnails_storage_path, "*.thumb")).each do |file|
273:       File.delete file
274:     end
275:   end
276: 
277:   def self.clear_markdownized_previews
278:     Dir.glob(File.join(markdownized_previews_storage_path, "*.md")).each do |file|
279:       File.delete file
280:     end
281:   end
282: 
283:   def is_text?
284:     Redmine::MimeType.is_type?('text', filename) || Redmine::SyntaxHighlighting.filename_supported?(filename)
285:   end
286: 
287:   def is_markdown?
288:     Redmine::MimeType.of(filename) == 'text/markdown'
289:   end
290: 
291:   def is_textile?
292:     Redmine::MimeType.of(filename) == 'text/x-textile'
293:   end
294: 
295:   def is_image?
296:     Redmine::MimeType.is_type?('image', filename)
297:   end
298: 
299:   def is_diff?
300:     /\.(patch|diff)$/i.match?(filename)
301:   end
302: 
303:   def is_pdf?
304:     Redmine::MimeType.of(filename) == "application/pdf"
305:   end
306: 
307:   def is_video?
308:     Redmine::MimeType.is_type?('video', filename)
309:   end
310: 
311:   def is_audio?
312:     Redmine::MimeType.is_type?('audio', filename)
313:   end
314: 
315:   def markdownized_previewable?
316:     readable? && Redmine::Markdownizer.available? && Redmine::Markdownizer.supports?(filename)
317:   end
318: 
319:   def markdownized_preview_content
320:     return nil unless markdownized_previewable?
321: 
322:     target = markdownized_preview_cache_path
323:     if Redmine::Markdownizer.convert(diskfile, target)
324:       File.read(target, :mode => "rb")
325:     end
326:   rescue => e
327:     if logger
328:       logger.error(
329:         "An error occured while generating markdownized preview for #{disk_filename} " \
330:           "to #{target}\nException was: #{e.message}"
331:       )
332:     end
333:     nil
334:   end
335: 
336:   def markdownized_preview_cache_path
337:     File.join(self.class.markdownized_previews_storage_path, "#{digest}_#{filesize}.md")
338:   end
339: 
340:   def previewable?
341:     is_text? || is_image? || is_video? || is_audio?
342:   end
343: 
344:   # Returns true if the file is readable
345:   def readable?
346:     disk_filename.present? && File.readable?(diskfile)
347:   end
348: 
349:   # Returns the attachment token
350:   def token
351:     "#{id}.#{digest}"
352:   end
353: 
354:   # Finds an attachment that matches the given token and that has no container
355:   def self.find_by_token(token)
356:     if token.to_s =~ /^(\d+)\.([0-9a-f]+)$/
357:       attachment_id, attachment_digest = $1, $2
358:       attachment = Attachment.find_by(:id => attachment_id, :digest => attachment_digest)
359:       if attachment && attachment.container.nil?
360:         attachment
361:       end
362:     end
363:   end
364: 
365:   # Bulk attaches a set of files to an object
366:   #
367:   # Returns a Hash of the results:
368:   # :files => array of the attached files
369:   # :unsaved => array of the files that could not be attached
370:   def self.attach_files(obj, attachments)
371:     result = obj.save_attachments(attachments, User.current)
372:     obj.attach_saved_attachments
373:     result
374:   end
375: 
376:   # Updates the filename and description of a set of attachments
377:   # with the given hash of attributes. Returns true if all
378:   # attachments were updated.
379:   #
380:   # Example:
381:   #   Attachment.update_attachments(attachments, {
382:   #     4 => {:filename => 'foo'},
383:   #     7 => {:filename => 'bar', :description => 'file description'}
384:   #   })
385:   #
386:   def self.update_attachments(attachments, params)
387:     converted = {}
388:     params.each {|key, val| converted[key.to_i] = val}
389:     saved = true
390:     transaction do
391:       attachments.each do |attachment|
392:         if file = converted[attachment.id]
393:           attachment.filename = file[:filename] if file.key?(:filename)
394:           attachment.description = file[:description] if file.key?(:description)
395:           saved &&= attachment.save
396:         end
397:       end
398:       unless saved
399:         raise ActiveRecord::Rollback
400:       end
401:     end
402:     saved
403:   end
404: 
405:   def self.latest_attach(attachments, filename)
406:     return unless filename.valid_encoding?
407: 
408:     attachments.sort_by{|attachment| [attachment.created_on, attachment.id]}.reverse.detect do |att|
409:       filename.casecmp?(att.filename)
410:     end
411:   end
412: 
413:   def self.prune(age=1.day)
414:     Attachment.where("created_on < ? AND (container_type IS NULL OR container_type = '')", Time.now - age).destroy_all
415:   end
416: 
417:   def self.archive_attachments(attachments)
418:     attachments = attachments.select(&:readable?)
419:     return nil if attachments.blank?
420: 
421:     Zip.unicode_names = true
422:     archived_file_names = []
423:     buffer = Zip::OutputStream.write_buffer do |zos|
424:       attachments.each do |attachment|
425:         filename = attachment.filename
426:         # rename the file if a file with the same name already exists
427:         dup_count = 0
428:         while archived_file_names.include?(filename)
429:           dup_count += 1
430:           extname = File.extname(attachment.filename)
431:           basename = File.basename(attachment.filename, extname)
432:           filename = "#{basename}(#{dup_count})#{extname}"
433:         end
434:         zos.put_next_entry(filename)
435:         zos << IO.binread(attachment.diskfile)
436:         archived_file_names << filename
437:       end
438:     end
439:     buffer.string
440:   ensure
441:     buffer&.close
442:   end
443: 
444:   # Moves an existing attachment to its target directory
445:   def move_to_target_directory!
446:     return unless !new_record? & readable?
447: 
448:     src = diskfile
449:     self.disk_directory = target_directory
450:     dest = diskfile
451: 
452:     return if src == dest
453: 
454:     unless FileUtils.mkdir_p(File.dirname(dest))
455:       logger.error "Could not create directory #{File.dirname(dest)}" if logger
456:       return
457:     end
458: 
459:     unless FileUtils.mv(src, dest)
460:       logger.error "Could not move attachment from #{src} to #{dest}" if logger
461:       return
462:     end
463: 
464:     update_column :disk_directory, disk_directory
465:   end
466: 
467:   # Moves existing attachments that are stored at the root of the files
468:   # directory (ie. created before Redmine 2.3) to their target subdirectories
469:   def self.move_from_root_to_target_directory
470:     Attachment.where("disk_directory IS NULL OR disk_directory = ''").find_each do |attachment|
471:       attachment.move_to_target_directory!
472:     end
473:   end
474: 
475:   # Updates digests to SHA256 for all attachments that have a MD5 digest
476:   # (ie. created before Redmine 3.4)
477:   def self.update_digests_to_sha256
478:     Attachment.where("length(digest) < 64").find_each do |attachment|
479:       attachment.update_digest_to_sha256!
480:     end
481:   end
482: 
483:   # Updates attachment digest to SHA256
484:   def update_digest_to_sha256!
485:     if readable?
486:       sha = Digest::SHA256.new
487:       File.open(diskfile, 'rb') do |f|
488:         while buffer = f.read(8192)
489:           sha.update(buffer)
490:         end
491:       end
492:       update_column :digest, sha.hexdigest
493:     end
494:   end
495: 
496:   # Returns true if the extension is allowed regarding allowed/denied
497:   # extensions defined in application settings, otherwise false
498:   def self.valid_extension?(extension)
499:     denied, allowed = [:attachment_extensions_denied, :attachment_extensions_allowed].map do |setting|
500:       Setting.send(setting)
501:     end
502:     if denied.present? && extension_in?(extension, denied)
503:       return false
504:     end
505:     if allowed.present? && !extension_in?(extension, allowed)
506:       return false
507:     end
508: 
509:     true
510:   end
511: 
512:   # Returns true if extension belongs to extensions list.
513:   def self.extension_in?(extension, extensions)
514:     extension = extension.downcase.sub(/\A\.+/, '')
515: 
516:     unless extensions.is_a?(Array)
517:       extensions = extensions.to_s.split(",").map(&:strip)
518:     end
519:     extensions = extensions.map {|s| s.downcase.sub(/\A\.+/, '')}.reject(&:blank?)
520:     extensions.include?(extension)
521:   end
522: 
523:   # Returns true if attachment's extension belongs to extensions list.
524:   def extension_in?(extensions)
525:     self.class.extension_in?(File.extname(filename), extensions)
526:   end
527: 
528:   # returns either MD5 or SHA256 depending on the way self.digest was computed
529:   def digest_type
530:     digest.size < 64 ? "MD5" : "SHA256" if digest.present?
531:   end
532: 
533:   private
534: 
535:   def reuse_existing_file_if_possible
536:     original_diskfile = diskfile
537:     original_filename = disk_filename
538:     reused = with_lock do
539:       if existing = Attachment
540:                       .where(digest: self.digest, filesize: self.filesize)
541:                       .where.not(disk_filename: original_filename)
542:                       .order(:id)
543:                       .last
544:         existing.with_lock do
545:           existing_diskfile = existing.diskfile
546:           if File.readable?(original_diskfile) &&
547:             File.readable?(existing_diskfile) &&
548:             FileUtils.identical?(original_diskfile, existing_diskfile)
549:             self.update_columns disk_directory: existing.disk_directory,
550:                                 disk_filename: existing.disk_filename
551:           end
552:         end
553:       end
554:     end
555:     if reused && Attachment.where(disk_filename: original_filename).none?
556:       File.delete(original_diskfile)
557:     end
558:   rescue ActiveRecord::StatementInvalid, ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
559:     # Catch and ignore lock errors. It is not critical if deduplication does
560:     # not happen, therefore we do not retry.
561:     # with_lock throws ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound if the record isnt there
562:     # anymore, thats why this is caught and ignored as well.
563:   end
564: 
565:   # Physically deletes the file from the file system
566:   def delete_from_disk!
567:     FileUtils.rm_f(diskfile) if disk_filename.present?
568:     Dir[thumbnail_path("*")].each do |thumb|
569:       File.delete(thumb)
570:     end
571:     FileUtils.rm_f(markdownized_preview_cache_path)
572:   end
573: 
574:   def thumbnail_path(size)
575:     File.join(self.class.thumbnails_storage_path,
576:               "#{digest}_#{filesize}_#{size}.thumb")
577:   end
578: 
579:   def sanitize_filename(value)
580:     # get only the filename, not the whole path
581:     just_filename = value.gsub(/\A.*(\\|\/)/m, '')
582: 
583:     # Finally, replace invalid characters with underscore
584:     just_filename.gsub(/[\/?%*:|"'<>\n\r]+/, '_')
585:   end
586: 
587:   # Returns the subdirectory in which the attachment will be saved
588:   def target_directory
589:     time = created_on || DateTime.now
590:     time.strftime("%Y/%m")
591:   end
592: 
593:   # Singleton class method is public
594:   class << self
595:     # Claims a unique ASCII or hashed filename, yields the open file handle
596:     def create_diskfile(filename, directory=nil, &)
597:       timestamp = DateTime.now.strftime("%y%m%d%H%M%S")
598:       ascii = ''
599:       if %r{^[a-zA-Z0-9_.-]*$}.match?(filename) && filename.length <= 50
600:         ascii = filename
601:       else
602:         ascii = ActiveSupport::Digest.hexdigest(filename)
603:         # keep the extension if any
604:         ascii << $1 if filename =~ %r{(\.[a-zA-Z0-9]+)$}
605:       end
606: 
607:       path = File.join storage_path, directory.to_s
608:       FileUtils.mkdir_p(path) unless File.directory?(path)
609:       begin
610:         name = "#{timestamp}_#{ascii}"
611:         File.open(
612:           File.join(path, name),
613:           flags: File::CREAT | File::EXCL | File::WRONLY,
614:           binmode: true,
615:           &
616:         )
617:       rescue Errno::EEXIST
618:         timestamp.succ!
619:         retry
620:       end
621:     end
622:   end
623: end

19: 
20: class Watcher < ApplicationRecord
21:   belongs_to :watchable, :polymorphic => true
22:   belongs_to :user, :class_name => 'Principal'
23: 
24:   validates_presence_of :user
25:   validates_uniqueness_of :user_id, :scope => [:watchable_type, :watchable_id], :case_sensitive => true
26:   validate :validate_user
27: 
28:   # Returns true if at least one object among objects is watched by user
29:   def self.any_watched?(objects, user)
30:     objects = objects.reject(&:new_record?)
31:     if objects.any?
32:       objects.group_by {|object| object.class.base_class}.each do |base_class, objects|
33:         if Watcher.where(:watchable_type => base_class.name, :watchable_id => objects.map(&:id), :user_id => user.id).exists?
34:           return true
35:         end
36:       end
37:     end
38:     false
39:   end
40: 
41:   # Unwatch things that users are no longer allowed to view
42:   def self.prune(options={})
43:     if options.has_key?(:user)
44:       prune_single_user(options[:user], options)
45:     else
46:       pruned = 0
47:       User.where("id IN (SELECT DISTINCT user_id FROM #{table_name})").each do |user|
48:         pruned += prune_single_user(user, options)
49:       end
50:       pruned
51:     end
52:   end
53: 
54:   protected
55: 
56:   def validate_user
57:     errors.add :user_id, :invalid \
58:       unless user.nil? || (user.is_a?(User) && user.active?) || (user.is_a?(Group) && user.givable?)
59:   end
60: 
61:   def self.prune_single_user(user, options={})
62:     return unless user.is_a?(User)
63: 
64:     pruned = 0
65:     where(:user_id => user.id).each do |watcher|
66:       next if watcher.watchable.nil?
67: 
68:       if options.has_key?(:project)
69:         unless watcher.watchable.respond_to?(:project) &&
70:                  watcher.watchable.project == options[:project]
71:           next
72:         end
73:       end
74:       if watcher.watchable.respond_to?(:visible?)
75:         unless watcher.watchable.visible?(user)
76:           watcher.destroy
77:           pruned += 1
78:         end
79:       end
80:     end
81:     pruned
82:   end
83:   private_class_method :prune_single_user
84: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: # Redmine - project management software

13: # MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the
14: # GNU General Public License for more details.
15: #
16: # You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License
17: # along with this program; if not, write to the Free Software
18: # Foundation, Inc., 51 Franklin Street, Fifth Floor, Boston, MA  02110-1301, USA.
19: 
20: class Changeset < ApplicationRecord
21:   belongs_to :repository
22:   belongs_to :user
23:   has_many :filechanges, :class_name => 'Change', :dependent => :delete_all
24:   has_and_belongs_to_many :issues
25:   has_and_belongs_to_many :parents,
26:                           :class_name => "Changeset",
27:                           :join_table => "#{table_name_prefix}changeset_parents#{table_name_suffix}",
28:                           :association_foreign_key => 'parent_id', :foreign_key => 'changeset_id'
29:   has_and_belongs_to_many :children,
30:                           :class_name => "Changeset",
31:                           :join_table => "#{table_name_prefix}changeset_parents#{table_name_suffix}",
32:                           :association_foreign_key => 'changeset_id', :foreign_key => 'parent_id'
33: 
34:   acts_as_event(
35:     :title => proc {|o| o.title},
36:     :description => :long_comments,
37:     :datetime => :committed_on,
38:     :url =>
39:       proc do |o|
40:         {:controller => 'repositories', :action => 'revision',
41:          :id => o.repository.project,
42:          :repository_id => o.repository.identifier_param, :rev => o.identifier}
43:       end
44:   )
45:   acts_as_searchable :columns => 'comments',
46:                      :preload => {:repository => :project},
47:                      :project_key => "#{Repository.table_name}.project_id",
48:                      :date_column => :committed_on
49: 
50:   acts_as_activity_provider :timestamp => "#{table_name}.committed_on",
51:                             :author_key => :user_id,
52:                             :scope => proc {preload(:user, {:repository => :project})}
53: 
54:   validates_presence_of :repository_id, :revision, :committed_on, :commit_date
55:   validates_uniqueness_of :revision, :scope => :repository_id, :case_sensitive => true
56:   validates_uniqueness_of :scmid, :scope => :repository_id, :allow_nil => true, :case_sensitive => true
57: 
58:   scope :visible, (lambda do |*args|
59:     joins(:repository => :project).
60:     where(Project.allowed_to_condition(args.shift || User.current, :view_changesets, *args))
61:   end)
62: 
63:   before_create :before_create_cs
64:   after_create :scan_for_issues
65: 
66:   def revision=(r)
67:     write_attribute :revision, (r.nil? ? nil : r.to_s)
68:   end
69: 
70:   # Returns the identifier of this changeset; depending on repository backends
71:   def identifier
72:     if repository.class.respond_to? :changeset_identifier
73:       repository.class.changeset_identifier self
74:     else
75:       revision.to_s
76:     end
77:   end
78: 
79:   def committed_on=(date)
80:     self.commit_date = date
81:     super
82:   end
83: 
84:   # Returns the readable identifier
85:   def format_identifier
86:     if repository.class.respond_to? :format_changeset_identifier
87:       repository.class.format_changeset_identifier self
88:     else
89:       identifier
90:     end
91:   end
92: 
93:   def project
94:     repository.project
95:   end
96: 
97:   def author
98:     user || committer.to_s.split('<').first
99:   end
100: 
101:   def before_create_cs
102:     self.committer = self.class.to_utf8(self.committer, repository.repo_log_encoding)
103:     self.comments =
104:       self.class.normalize_comments(self.comments, repository.repo_log_encoding)
105:     self.user = repository.find_committer_user(self.committer)
106:   end
107: 
108:   def scan_for_issues
109:     scan_comment_for_issue_ids
110:   end
111: 
112:   TIMELOG_RE = /
113:     (
114:     ((\d+)(h|hours?))((\d+)(m|min)?)?
115:     |
116:     ((\d+)(h|hours?|m|min))
117:     |
118:     (\d+):(\d+)
119:     |
120:     (\d+([.,]\d+)?)h?
121:     )
122:     /x
123: 
124:   def scan_comment_for_issue_ids
125:     return if comments.blank?
126: 
127:     # keywords used to reference issues
128:     ref_keywords = Setting.commit_ref_keywords.downcase.split(",").collect(&:strip)
129:     ref_keywords_any = ref_keywords.delete('*')
130: 
131:     # keywords used to fix issues
132:     fix_keywords = Setting.commit_update_keywords_array.pluck('keywords').flatten.compact
133:     kw_regexp = (ref_keywords + fix_keywords).collect{|kw| Regexp.escape(kw)}.join("|")
134: 
135:     referenced_issues = []
136:     regexp =
137:       %r{
138:         ([\s(\[,-]|^)((#{kw_regexp})[\s:]+)?
139:         (\#\d+(\s+@#{TIMELOG_RE})?([\s,;&]+\#\d+(\s+@#{TIMELOG_RE})?)*)
140:         (?=[[:punct:]]|\s|<|$)
141:       }xi
142:     comments.scan(regexp) do |match|
143:       action = match[2].to_s.downcase
144:       refs   = match[3]
145:       next unless action.present? || ref_keywords_any
146: 
147:       refs.scan(/#(\d+)(\s+@#{TIMELOG_RE})?/o).each do |m|
148:         issue = find_referenced_issue_by_id(m[0].to_i)
149:         hours = m[2]
150:         if issue && !issue_linked_to_same_commit?(issue)
151:           referenced_issues << issue
152:           # Don't update issues or log time when importing old commits
153:           unless repository.created_on && committed_on && committed_on < repository.created_on
154:             fix_issue(issue, action) if fix_keywords.include?(action)
155:             log_time(issue, hours) if hours && Setting.commit_logtime_enabled?
156:           end
157:         end
158:       end
159:     end
160: 
161:     referenced_issues.uniq!
162:     self.issues = referenced_issues unless referenced_issues.empty?
163:   end
164: 
165:   def short_comments
166:     @short_comments || split_comments.first
167:   end
168: 
169:   def long_comments
170:     @long_comments || split_comments.last
171:   end
172: 
173:   def text_tag(ref_project=nil)
174:     repo = ""
175:     if repository && repository.identifier.present?
176:       repo = "#{repository.identifier}|"
177:     end
178:     tag = scmid? ? "commit:#{repo}#{scmid}" : "#{repo}r#{revision}"
179:     if ref_project && project && ref_project != project
180:       tag = "#{project.identifier}:#{tag}"
181:     end
182:     tag
183:   end
184: 
185:   # Returns the title used for the changeset in the activity/search results
186:   def title
187:     repo = (repository && repository.identifier.present?) ? " (#{repository.identifier})" : ''
188:     comm = short_comments.blank? ? '' : (': ' + short_comments)
189:     "#{l(:label_revision)} #{format_identifier}#{repo}#{comm}"
190:   end
191: 
192:   # Returns the previous changeset
193:   def previous
194:     @previous ||= repository&.previous_changeset(self)
195:   end
196: 
197:   # Returns the next changeset
198:   def next
199:     @next ||= repository&.next_changeset(self)
200:   end
201: 
202:   # Creates a new Change from it's common parameters
203:   def create_change(change)
204:     Change.create(:changeset     => self,
205:                   :action        => change[:action],
206:                   :path          => change[:path],
207:                   :from_path     => change[:from_path],
208:                   :from_revision => change[:from_revision])
209:   end
210: 
211:   # Finds an issue that can be referenced by the commit message
212:   def find_referenced_issue_by_id(id)
213:     return nil if id.blank?
214: 
215:     issue = Issue.find_by_id(id.to_i)
216:     if Setting.commit_cross_project_ref?
217:       # all issues can be referenced/fixed
218:     elsif issue
219:       # issue that belong to the repository project, a subproject or a parent project only
220:       unless issue.project &&
221:                 (project == issue.project || project.is_ancestor_of?(issue.project) ||
222:                  project.is_descendant_of?(issue.project))
223:         issue = nil
224:       end
225:     end
226:     issue
227:   end
228: 
229:   private
230: 
231:   # Returns true if the issue is already linked to the same commit
232:   # from a different repository
233:   def issue_linked_to_same_commit?(issue)
234:     repository.same_commits_in_scope(issue.changesets, self).any?
235:   end
236: 
237:   # Updates the +issue+ according to +action+
238:   def fix_issue(issue, action)
239:     # the issue may have been updated by the closure of another one (eg. duplicate)
240:     issue.reload
241:     # don't change the status is the issue is closed
242:     return if issue.closed?
243: 
244:     journal = issue.init_journal(user || User.anonymous,
245:                                  ll(Setting.default_language,
246:                                     :text_status_changed_by_changeset,
247:                                     text_tag(issue.project)))
248:     rule = Setting.commit_update_keywords_array.detect do |rule|
249:       rule['keywords'].include?(action) &&
250:         (rule['if_tracker_id'].blank? || rule['if_tracker_id'] == issue.tracker_id.to_s)
251:     end
252:     if rule
253:       issue.assign_attributes rule.slice(*Issue.attribute_names)
254:     end
255:     Redmine::Hook.call_hook(:model_changeset_scan_commit_for_issue_ids_pre_issue_update,
256:                             {:changeset => self, :issue => issue, :action => action})
257: 
258:     if issue.changes.any?
259:       unless issue.save
260:         logger.warn("Issue ##{issue.id} could not be saved by changeset #{id}: #{issue.errors.full_messages}") if logger
261:       end
262:     else
263:       issue.clear_journal
264:     end
265:     issue
266:   end
267: 
268:   def log_time(issue, hours)
269:     time_entry =
270:       TimeEntry.new(
271:         :user => user,
272:         :hours => hours,
273:         :issue => issue,
274:         :spent_on => commit_date,
275:         :comments => l(:text_time_logged_by_changeset, :value => text_tag(issue.project),
276:                        :locale => Setting.default_language)
277:       )
278:     if activity = issue.project.commit_logtime_activity
279:       time_entry.activity = activity
280:     end
281: 
282:     unless time_entry.save
283:       logger.warn("TimeEntry could not be created by changeset #{id}: #{time_entry.errors.full_messages}") if logger
284:     end
285:     time_entry
286:   end
287: 
288:   def split_comments
289:     comments =~ /\A(.+?)\r?\n(.*)$/m
290:     @short_comments = $1 || comments
291:     @long_comments = $2.to_s.strip
292:     [@short_comments, @long_comments]
293:   end
294: 
295:   # Singleton class method is public
296:   class << self
297:     # Strips and reencodes a commit log before insertion into the database
298:     def normalize_comments(str, encoding)
299:       Changeset.to_utf8(str.to_s, encoding).strip
300:     end
301: 
302:     def to_utf8(str, encoding)
303:       Redmine::CodesetUtil.to_utf8(str, encoding)
304:     end
305:   end
306: end

(End of file - total 306 lines)

20: class Project < ApplicationRecord
21:   include Redmine::SafeAttributes
22:   include Redmine::NestedSet::ProjectNestedSet
23: 
24:   # Project statuses
25:   STATUS_ACTIVE     = 1
26:   STATUS_CLOSED     = 5
27:   STATUS_ARCHIVED   = 9
28:   STATUS_SCHEDULED_FOR_DELETION = 10
29: 
30:   # Maximum length for project identifiers
31:   IDENTIFIER_MAX_LENGTH = 100
32: 
33:   has_many :memberships, :class_name => 'Member', :inverse_of => :project
34:   # Memberships of active users only
35:   has_many :members,
36:            lambda {joins(:principal).where(:users => {:type => 'User', :status => Principal::STATUS_ACTIVE})}
37:   has_many :users, through: :members
38:   has_many :enabled_modules, :dependent => :delete_all
39:   has_and_belongs_to_many :trackers, lambda {order(:position)}
40:   has_many :issues, :dependent => :destroy
41:   has_many :issue_changes, :through => :issues, :source => :journals
42:   has_many :versions, :dependent => :destroy
43:   belongs_to :default_version, :class_name => 'Version'
44:   belongs_to :default_assigned_to, :class_name => 'Principal'
45:   has_many :time_entries, :dependent => :destroy
46:   # Specific overridden Activities
47:   has_many :time_entry_activities, :dependent => :destroy
48:   has_many :queries, :dependent => :destroy
49:   has_many :documents, :dependent => :destroy
50:   has_many :news, lambda {includes(:author)}, :dependent => :destroy
51:   has_many :issue_categories, lambda {order(:name)}, :dependent => :delete_all
52:   has_many :boards, lambda {order(:position)}, :inverse_of => :project, :dependent => :destroy
53:   has_one :repository, lambda {where(:is_default => true)}
54:   has_many :repositories, :dependent => :destroy
55:   has_many :changesets, :through => :repository
56:   has_one :wiki, :dependent => :destroy
57:   # Custom field for the project issues
58:   has_and_belongs_to_many :issue_custom_fields,
59:                           lambda {order(:position)},
60:                           :class_name => 'IssueCustomField',
61:                           :join_table => "#{table_name_prefix}custom_fields_projects#{table_name_suffix}",
62:                           :association_foreign_key => 'custom_field_id'
63:   has_and_belongs_to_many :webhooks
64: 
65:   # Default Custom Query
66:   belongs_to :default_issue_query, :class_name => 'IssueQuery'
67: 
68:   acts_as_attachable :view_permission => :view_files,
69:                      :edit_permission => :manage_files,
70:                      :delete_permission => :manage_files
71: 
72:   acts_as_customizable
73:   acts_as_searchable :columns => ['name', 'identifier', 'description'],
74:                      :project_key => "#{Project.table_name}.id",
75:                      :permission => nil
76:   acts_as_event :title => proc {|o| "#{l(:label_project)}: #{o.name}"},
77:                 :url => proc {|o| {:controller => 'projects', :action => 'show', :id => o}},
78:                 :author => nil
79: 
80:   validates_presence_of :name, :identifier
81:   validates_uniqueness_of :identifier, :if => proc {|p| p.identifier_changed?}, :case_sensitive => true
82:   validates_length_of :name, :maximum => 255
83:   validates_length_of :homepage, :maximum => 255
84:   validates_length_of :identifier, :maximum => IDENTIFIER_MAX_LENGTH
85:   # downcase letters, digits, dashes but not digits only
86:   validates_format_of :identifier, :with => /\A(?!\d+$)[a-z0-9\-_]*\z/,
87:                       :if => proc {|p| p.identifier_changed?}
88:   # reserved words
89:   validates_exclusion_of :identifier,
90:                          :in => %w(new autocomplete bulk_destroy),
91:                          :if => -> { new_record? || will_save_change_to_identifier? }
92:   validate :validate_parent
93: 
94:   after_update :update_versions_from_hierarchy_change,
95:                :if => proc {|project| project.saved_change_to_parent_id?}
96:   before_destroy :delete_all_members
97:   after_save :update_inherited_members,
98:              :if => proc {|project| project.saved_change_to_inherit_members?}
99:   after_save :remove_inherited_member_roles, :add_inherited_member_roles,
100:              :if => proc {|project| project.saved_change_to_parent_id?}
101: 
102:   scope :has_module, (lambda do |mod|
103:     where("#{Project.table_name}.id IN (SELECT em.project_id FROM #{EnabledModule.table_name} em WHERE em.name=?)", mod.to_s)
104:   end)
105:   scope :active, lambda {where(:status => STATUS_ACTIVE)}
106:   scope :status, lambda {|arg| where(arg.blank? ? nil : {:status => arg.to_i})}
107:   scope :all_public, lambda {where(:is_public => true)}
108:   scope :visible, lambda {|*args| where(Project.visible_condition(args.shift || User.current, *args))}
109:   scope :allowed_to, (lambda do |*args|
110:     user = args.first.is_a?(Symbol) ? User.current : args.shift
111:     permission = args.shift
112:     where(Project.allowed_to_condition(user, permission, *args))
113:   end)
114:   scope :like, (lambda do |arg|
115:     if arg.present?
116:       pattern = "%#{sanitize_sql_like arg.to_s.strip}%"
117:       where("LOWER(#{Project.table_name}.identifier) LIKE LOWER(:p) ESCAPE :s OR LOWER(#{Project.table_name}.name) LIKE LOWER(:p) ESCAPE :s", :p => pattern, :s => '\\')
118:     end
119:   end)
120:   scope :sorted, lambda {order(:lft)}
121:   scope :having_trackers, (lambda do
122:     where("#{Project.table_name}.id IN (SELECT DISTINCT project_id FROM #{table_name_prefix}projects_trackers#{table_name_suffix})")
123:   end)
124: 
125:   def initialize(attributes=nil, *args)
126:     super
127: 
128:     # rubocop:disable Style/NegatedIf
129:     initialized = (attributes || {}).stringify_keys
130:     if !initialized.key?('identifier') && Setting.sequential_project_identifiers?
131:       self.identifier = Project.next_identifier
132:     end
133:     if !initialized.key?('is_public')
134:       self.is_public = Setting.default_projects_public?
135:     end
136:     if !initialized.key?('enabled_module_names')
137:       self.enabled_module_names = Setting.default_projects_modules
138:     end
139:     if !initialized.key?('trackers') && !initialized.key?('tracker_ids')
140:       default = Setting.default_projects_tracker_ids
141:       if default.is_a?(Array)
142:         self.trackers = Tracker.where(:id => default.map(&:to_i)).sorted.to_a
143:       else
144:         self.trackers = Tracker.sorted.to_a
145:       end
146:     end
147:     # rubocop:enable Style/NegatedIf
148:   end
149: 
150:   def identifier=(identifier)
151:     super unless identifier_frozen?
152:   end

172:   # Returns a SQL conditions string used to find all projects visible by the specified user.
173:   #
174:   # Examples:
175:   #   Project.visible_condition(admin)        => "projects.status = 1"
176:   #   Project.visible_condition(normal_user)  => "((projects.status = 1) AND (projects.is_public = 1 OR projects.id IN (1,3,4)))"
177:   #   Project.visible_condition(anonymous)    => "((projects.status = 1) AND (projects.is_public = 1))"
178:   def self.visible_condition(user, options={})
179:     allowed_to_condition(user, :view_project, options)
180:   end
181: 
182:   # Returns a SQL conditions string used to find all projects for which +user+ has the given +permission+
183:   #
184:   # Valid options:
185:   # * :skip_pre_condition => true       don't check that the module is enabled (eg. when the condition is already set elsewhere in the query)
186:   # * :project => project               limit the condition to project
187:   # * :with_subprojects => true         limit the condition to project and its subprojects
188:   # * :member => true                   limit the condition to the user projects
189:   def self.allowed_to_condition(user, permission, options={})
190:     perm = Redmine::AccessControl.permission(permission)
191:     base_statement =
192:       if perm && perm.read?
193:         "#{Project.table_name}.status IN (#{Project::STATUS_ACTIVE}, #{Project::STATUS_CLOSED})"
194:       else
195:         "#{Project.table_name}.status = #{Project::STATUS_ACTIVE}"
196:       end
197:     if !options[:skip_pre_condition] && perm && perm.project_module
198:       # If the permission belongs to a project module, make sure the module is enabled
199:       base_statement +=
200:         " AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 AS one FROM #{EnabledModule.table_name} em" \
201:           " WHERE em.project_id = #{Project.table_name}.id" \
202:           " AND em.name='#{perm.project_module}')"
203:     end
204:     if project = options[:project]
205:       project_statement = project.project_condition(options[:with_subprojects])
206:       base_statement = "(#{project_statement}) AND (#{base_statement})"
207:     end
208: 
209:     if user.admin?
210:       base_statement
211:     else
212:       statement_by_role = {}
213:       unless options[:member]
214:         role = user.builtin_role
215:         if role.allowed_to?(permission)
216:           s = "#{Project.table_name}.is_public = #{connection.quoted_true}"
217:           if user.id
218:             group = role.anonymous? ? Group.anonymous : Group.non_member
219:             principal_ids = [user.id, group.id].compact
220:             s =
221:               "(#{s} AND #{Project.table_name}.id NOT IN " \
222:                 "(SELECT project_id FROM #{Member.table_name} " \
223:                 "WHERE user_id IN (#{principal_ids.join(',')})))"
224:           end
225:           statement_by_role[role] = s
226:         end
227:       end
228:       user.project_ids_by_role.each do |role, project_ids|
229:         if role.allowed_to?(permission) && project_ids.any?
230:           statement_by_role[role] = "#{Project.table_name}.id IN (#{project_ids.join(',')})"
231:         end
232:       end
233:       if statement_by_role.empty?
234:         "1=0"
235:       else
236:         if block_given?
237:           statement_by_role.each do |role, statement|
238:             if s = yield(role, user)
239:               statement_by_role[role] = "(#{statement} AND (#{s}))"
240:             end
241:           end
242:         end
243:         "((#{base_statement}) AND (#{statement_by_role.values.join(' OR ')}))"
244:       end
245:     end
246:   end

248:   def override_roles(role)
249:     @override_members ||= memberships.
250:       joins(:principal).
251:       where(:users => {:type => ['GroupAnonymous', 'GroupNonMember']}).to_a
252: 
253:     group_class = role.anonymous? ? GroupAnonymous : GroupNonMember
254:     member = @override_members.detect {|m| m.principal.is_a? group_class}
255:     member ? member.roles.to_a : [role]
256:   end

271:     scope = TimeEntryActivity.where("#{t}.project_id IS NULL OR #{t}.project_id = ?", id)

283:   # Creates or updates project time entry activities

286:       activities.each do |id, activity|

292:   # Will create a new Project specific Activity or update an existing one
293:   #
294:   # This will raise a ActiveRecord::Rollback if the TimeEntryActivity
295:   # does not successfully save.
296:   def update_or_create_time_entry_activity(id, activity_hash)
297:     if activity_hash.respond_to?(:has_key?) && activity_hash.has_key?('parent_id')
298:       self.create_time_entry_activity_if_needed(activity_hash)
299:     else
300:       activity = project.time_entry_activities.find_by_id(id.to_i)
301:       activity.update(activity_hash) if activity
302:     end
303:   end
304: 
305:   # Create a new TimeEntryActivity if it overrides a system TimeEntryActivity
306:   #
307:   # This will raise a ActiveRecord::Rollback if the TimeEntryActivity
308:   # does not successfully save.
309:   def create_time_entry_activity_if_needed(activity)
310:     if activity['parent_id']
311:       parent_activity = TimeEntryActivity.find(activity['parent_id'])
312:       activity['name'] = parent_activity.name
313:       activity['position'] = parent_activity.position
314:       if Enumeration.overriding_change?(activity, parent_activity)
315:         project_activity = self.time_entry_activities.create(activity)
316:         if project_activity.new_record?
317:           raise ActiveRecord::Rollback, "Overriding TimeEntryActivity was not successfully saved"
318:         else
319:           self.time_entries.
320:             where(:activity_id => parent_activity.id).
321:             update_all(:activity_id => project_activity.id)
322:         end
323:       end
324:     end
325:   end

417:   # Archives the project and its descendants
418:   def archive
419:     # Check that there is no issue of a non descendant project that is assigned
420:     # to one of the project or descendant versions
421:     version_ids = self_and_descendants.joins(:versions).pluck("#{Version.table_name}.id")
422: 
423:     if version_ids.any? &&
424:       Issue.
425:         joins(:project).
426:         where("#{Project.table_name}.lft < ? OR #{Project.table_name}.rgt > ?", lft, rgt).
427:         where(:fixed_version_id => version_ids).
428:         exists?
429:       return false
430:     end
431: 
432:     Project.transaction do
433:       archive!
434:     end
435:     true
436:   end

439:   def unarchive
440:     new_status = ancestors.any?(&:closed?) ? STATUS_CLOSED : STATUS_ACTIVE
441:     self_and_ancestors.status(STATUS_ARCHIVED).update_all :status => new_status
442:     reload
443:   end

459:     @allowed_parents = @allowed_parents - self_and_descendants
460:     if user.allowed_to?(:add_project, nil, :global => true) || (!new_record? && parent.nil?)
461:       @allowed_parents << nil
462:     end
463:     unless parent.nil? || @allowed_parents.empty? || @allowed_parents.include?(parent)
464:       @allowed_parents << parent
465:     end

540:         where("#{Project.table_name}.status <> ? AND #{Version.table_name}.sharing = 'system'",
541:               STATUS_ARCHIVED)
542:     else
543:       @shared_versions ||= begin

545:         Version.
546:           joins(:project).
547:           preload(:project).
548:           where(

553:             " AND #{Project.table_name}.rgt <= #{r.rgt}" \
554:             " AND #{Version.table_name}.sharing = 'tree')" \
555:             " OR (#{Project.table_name}.lft < #{lft}" \
556:             " AND #{Project.table_name}.rgt > #{rgt}" \
557:             " AND #{Version.table_name}.sharing IN ('hierarchy', 'descendants'))" \
558:             " OR (#{Project.table_name}.lft > #{lft}" \
559:             " AND #{Project.table_name}.rgt < #{rgt}" \
560:             " AND #{Version.table_name}.sharing = 'hierarchy')" \
561:             "))"
562:           )
563:       end

603:   # Return a Principal scope of users/groups issues can be assigned to
604:   def assignable_users(tracker=nil)
605:     return @assignable_users[tracker] if @assignable_users && @assignable_users[tracker]
606: 
607:     types = ['User']
608:     types << 'Group' if Setting.issue_group_assignment?
609: 
610:     scope = Principal.
611:       active.
612:       joins(:members => :roles).
613:       where(:type => types, :members => {:project_id => id}, :roles => {:assignable => true}).
614:       distinct.
615:       sorted
616: 
617:     if tracker
618:       # Rejects users that cannot the view the tracker
619:       roles =
620:         Role.where(:assignable => true).select do |role|
621:           role.permissions_tracker?(:view_issues, tracker)
622:         end
623:       scope = scope.where(:roles => {:id => roles.map(&:id)})
624:     end
625: 
626:     @assignable_users ||= {}
627:     @assignable_users[tracker] = scope

731:   # Returns the percent completed for this project, based on the
732:   # progress on it's versions.
733:   def completed_percent(options={:include_subprojects => false})
734:     if options.delete(:include_subprojects)
735:       total = self_and_descendants.sum(&:completed_percent)
736: 
737:       total / self_and_descendants.count
738:     else
739:       if versions.count > 0
740:         total = versions.sum(&:completed_percent)
741: 
742:         total / versions.count
743:       else
744:         100
745:       end
746:     end

749:   # Return true if this project allows to do the specified action.
750:   # action can be:
751:   # * a parameter-like Hash (eg. :controller => 'projects', :action => 'edit')
752:   # * a permission Symbol (eg. :edit_project)
753:   def allows_to?(action)
754:     if archived?
755:       # No action allowed on archived projects
756:       return false
757:     end
758:     unless active? || Redmine::AccessControl.read_action?(action)
759:       # No write action allowed on closed projects
760:       return false
761:     end
762: 
763:     # No action allowed on disabled modules
764:     if action.is_a? Hash
765:       allowed_actions.include? "#{action[:controller]}/#{action[:action]}"
766:     else
767:       allowed_permissions.include? action
768:     end
769:   end

791:   def enabled_module_names=(module_names)
792:     if module_names && module_names.is_a?(Array)
793:       module_names = module_names.collect(&:to_s).reject(&:blank?)
794:       self.enabled_modules =
795:         module_names.collect do |name|
796:           enabled_modules.detect {|mod| mod.name == name} ||
797:             EnabledModule.new(:name => name)
798:         end
799:     else
800:       enabled_modules.clear
801:     end
802:   end
803: 
804:   # Returns an array of the enabled modules names
805:   def enabled_module_names
806:     enabled_modules.collect(&:name)
807:   end

809:   # Enable a specific module
810:   #
811:   # Examples:
812:   #   project.enable_module!(:issue_tracking)
813:   #   project.enable_module!("issue_tracking")
814:   def enable_module!(name)
815:     enabled_modules << EnabledModule.new(:name => name.to_s) unless module_enabled?(name)
816:   end
817: 
818:   # Disable a module if it exists
819:   #
820:   # Examples:
821:   #   project.disable_module!(:issue_tracking)
822:   #   project.disable_module!("issue_tracking")
823:   #   project.disable_module!(project.enabled_modules.first)
824:   def disable_module!(target)
825:     target = enabled_modules.detect{|mod| target.to_s == mod.name} unless enabled_modules.include?(target)
826:     (target.presence&.destroy)
827:   end

879:   def safe_attributes=(attrs, user=User.current)
880:     if attrs.respond_to?(:to_unsafe_hash)
881:       attrs = attrs.to_unsafe_hash
882:     end
883:     return unless attrs.is_a?(Hash)
884: 
885:     attrs = attrs.deep_dup
886: 
887:     @unallowed_parent_id = nil
888:     if new_record? || attrs.key?('parent_id')
889:       parent_id_param = attrs['parent_id'].to_s
890:       if new_record? || parent_id_param != parent_id.to_s
891:         p = parent_id_param.present? ? Project.find_by_id(parent_id_param) : nil
892:         unless allowed_parents(user).include?(p)
893:           attrs.delete('parent_id')
894:           @unallowed_parent_id = true
895:         end
896:       end
897:     end
898: 
899:     # Reject custom fields values not visible by the user
900:     if attrs['custom_field_values'].present?
901:       editable_custom_field_ids = editable_custom_field_values(user).map {|v| v.custom_field_id.to_s}
902:       attrs['custom_field_values'].reject! {|k, v| !editable_custom_field_ids.include?(k.to_s)}
903:     end
904: 
905:     # Reject custom fields not visible by the user
906:     if attrs['custom_fields'].present?
907:       editable_custom_field_ids = editable_custom_field_values(user).map {|v| v.custom_field_id.to_s}
908:       attrs['custom_fields'].reject! {|c| !editable_custom_field_ids.include?(c['id'].to_s)}
909:     end
910: 
911:     super
912:   end

914:   # Returns an auto-generated project identifier based on the last identifier used

917:     p.nil? ? nil : p.identifier.to_s.succ
918:   end

920:   # Copies and saves the Project instance based on the +project+.
921:   # Duplicates the source project's:
922:   # * Wiki
923:   # * Versions
924:   # * Categories
925:   # * Issues
926:   # * Members
927:   # * Queries
928:   #
929:   # Accepts an +options+ argument to specify what to copy
930:   #
931:   # Examples:
932:   #   project.copy(1)                                    # => copies everything
933:   #   project.copy(1, :only => 'members')                # => copies members only
934:   #   project.copy(1, :only => ['members', 'versions'])  # => copies members and versions
935:   def copy(project, options={})
936:     project = Project.find(project) unless project.is_a?(Project)
937: 
938:     to_be_copied = %w(members wiki versions issue_categories issues queries boards documents)
939:     to_be_copied = to_be_copied & Array.wrap(options[:only]) unless options[:only].nil?
940: 
941:     Project.transaction do
942:       if save
943:         reload
944: 
945:         self.attachments = project.attachments.map do |attachment|
946:           attachment.copy(:container => self)
947:         end
948: 
949:         to_be_copied.each do |name|
950:           send :"copy_#{name}", project
951:         end
952:         Redmine::Hook.call_hook(:model_project_copy_before_save,
953:                                 :source_project => project,
954:                                 :destination_project => self)
955:         save
956:       else
957:         false
958:       end
959:     end
960:   end

962:   # Returns a new unsaved Project instance with attributes copied from +project+
963:   def self.copy_from(project)
964:     project = Project.find(project) unless project.is_a?(Project)
965:     # clear unique attributes
966:     attributes =
967:       project.attributes.dup.except('id', 'name', 'identifier',
968:                                     'status', 'parent_id', 'lft', 'rgt')
969:     copy = Project.new(attributes)
970:     copy.enabled_module_names = project.enabled_module_names
971:     copy.trackers = project.trackers
972:     copy.custom_values = project.custom_values.collect {|v| v.clone}
973:     copy.issue_custom_fields = project.issue_custom_fields
974:     copy
975:   end

977:   # Yields the given block for each project with its level in the tree
978:   def self.project_tree(projects, options={}, &block)
979:     ancestors = []
980:     if options[:init_level] && projects.first
981:       ancestors = projects.first.ancestors.to_a
982:     end
983:     projects.sort_by(&:lft).each do |project|
984:       while ancestors.any? &&
985:              !project.is_descendant_of?(ancestors.last)
986:         ancestors.pop
987:       end
988:       yield project, ancestors.size
989:       ancestors << project
990:     end
991:   end

993:   # Overrides Redmine::Acts::Customizable::InstanceMethods#validate_custom_field_values
994:   # so that custom values that are not editable are not validated (eg. a custom field that
995:   # is marked as required should not trigger a validation error if the user is not allowed
996:   # to edit this field).
997:   def validate_custom_field_values
998:     user = User.current
999:     if new_record? || custom_field_values_changed?
1000:       editable_custom_field_values(user).each(&:validate_value)
1001:     end
1002:   end

1010:     user ||= User.current

1013:     end

1032:   def update_inherited_members
1033:     if parent
1034:       if inherit_members? && !inherit_members_before_last_save
1035:         remove_inherited_member_roles
1036:         add_inherited_member_roles
1037:       elsif !inherit_members? && inherit_members_before_last_save
1038:         remove_inherited_member_roles
1039:       end
1040:     end
1041:   end

1043:   def remove_inherited_member_roles
1044:     member_roles = MemberRole.where(:member_id => membership_ids).to_a
1045:     member_role_ids = member_roles.map(&:id)
1046:     member_roles.each do |member_role|
1047:       if member_role.inherited_from && !member_role_ids.include?(member_role.inherited_from)
1048:         member_role.destroy
1049:       end
1050:     end
1051:   end
1052: 
1053:   def add_inherited_member_roles
1054:     if inherit_members? && parent
1055:       parent.memberships.each do |parent_member|
1056:         member = Member.find_or_initialize_by(:project_id => self.id, :user_id => parent_member.user_id)
1057:         parent_member.member_roles.each do |parent_member_role|
1058:           member.member_roles <<
1059:             MemberRole.new(:role => parent_member_role.role,
1060:                            :inherited_from => parent_member_role.id)
1061:         end
1062:         member.save!
1063:       end
1064:       memberships.reset
1065:     end
1066:   end

1082:   # Copies wiki from +project+
1083:   def copy_wiki(project)
1084:     # Check that the source project has a wiki first
1085:     unless project.wiki.nil?
1086:       wiki = self.wiki || Wiki.new
1087:       wiki.attributes = project.wiki.attributes.dup.except("id", "project_id")
1088:       wiki_pages_map = {}
1089:       project.wiki.pages.each do |page|
1090:         # Skip pages without content
1091:         next if page.content.nil?
1092: 
1093:         new_wiki_content =
1094:           WikiContent.new(page.content.attributes.dup.except("id", "page_id", "updated_on"))
1095:         new_wiki_page =
1096:           WikiPage.new(page.attributes.dup.except("id", "wiki_id", "created_on", "parent_id"))
1097:         new_wiki_page.content = new_wiki_content
1098:         wiki.pages << new_wiki_page
1099:         new_wiki_page.attachments =
1100:           page.attachments.map{|attachement| attachement.copy(:container => new_wiki_page)}
1101:         wiki_pages_map[page.id] = new_wiki_page
1102:       end
1103: 
1104:       self.wiki = wiki
1105:       wiki.save
1106:       # Reproduce page hierarchy
1107:       project.wiki.pages.each do |page|
1108:         if page.parent_id && wiki_pages_map[page.id]
1109:           wiki_pages_map[page.id].parent = wiki_pages_map[page.parent_id]
1110:           wiki_pages_map[page.id].save
1111:         end
1112:       end
1113:     end
1114:   end

1121:         version.attributes.dup.except("id", "project_id", "created_on", "updated_on")
1122: 
1123:       new_version.attachments = version.attachments.map do |attachment|
1124:         attachment.copy(:container => new_version)
1125:       end
1126: 
1127:       self.versions << new_version
1128:     end
1129:   end

1132:   def copy_issue_categories(project)

1134:       new_issue_category = IssueCategory.new
1135:       new_issue_category.attributes = issue_category.attributes.dup.except("id", "project_id")
1136:       self.issue_categories << new_issue_category
1137:     end

1140:   # Copies issues from +project+
1141:   def copy_issues(project)
1142:     # Stores the source issue id as a key and the copied issues as the
1143:     # value.  Used to map the two together for issue relations.
1144:     issues_map = {}
1145: 
1146:     # Store status and reopen locked/closed versions
1147:     version_statuses = versions.reject(&:open?).map {|version| [version, version.status]}
1148:     version_statuses.each do |version, _| # rubocop:disable Style/HashEachMethods
1149:       version.update_attribute :status, 'open'
1150:     end
1151: 
1152:     # Get issues sorted by root_id, lft so that parent issues
1153:     # get copied before their children
1154:     project.issues.reorder('root_id, lft').each do |issue|
1155:       new_issue = Issue.new
1156:       new_issue.copy_from(issue, :subtasks => false, :link => false, :keep_status => true)
1157:       new_issue.project = self
1158:       # Changing project resets the custom field values
1159:       # TODO: handle this in Issue#project=
1160:       new_issue.custom_field_values = issue.custom_field_values.to_h do |v|
1161:         [v.custom_field_id, v.value]
1162:       end
1163:       # Reassign fixed_versions by name, since names are unique per project
1164:       if issue.fixed_version && issue.fixed_version.project == project
1165:         new_issue.fixed_version = self.versions.detect {|v| v.name == issue.fixed_version.name}
1166:       end
1167:       # Reassign version custom field values
1168:       new_issue.custom_field_values.each do |custom_value|
1169:         if custom_value.custom_field.field_format == 'version' && custom_value.value.present?
1170:           versions = Version.where(:id => custom_value.value).to_a
1171:           new_value = versions.map do |version|
1172:             if version.project == project
1173:               self.versions.detect {|v| v.name == version.name}.try(:id)
1174:             else
1175:               version.id
1176:             end
1177:           end
1178:           new_value.compact!
1179:           new_value = new_value.first unless custom_value.custom_field.multiple?
1180:           custom_value.value = new_value
1181:         end
1182:       end
1183:       # Reassign the category by name, since names are unique per project
1184:       if issue.category
1185:         new_issue.category = self.issue_categories.detect {|c| c.name == issue.category.name}
1186:       end
1187:       # Parent issue
1188:       if issue.parent_id
1189:         if copied_parent = issues_map[issue.parent_id]
1190:           new_issue.parent_issue_id = copied_parent.id
1191:         end
1192:       end
1193: 
1194:       self.issues << new_issue
1195:       if new_issue.new_record?
1196:         if logger && logger.info?
1197:           logger.info(
1198:             "Project#copy_issues: issue ##{issue.id} could not be copied: " \
1199:               "#{new_issue.errors.full_messages}"
1200:           )
1201:         end
1202:       else
1203:         issues_map[issue.id] = new_issue unless new_issue.new_record?
1204:       end
1205:     end
1206: 
1207:     # Restore locked/closed version statuses
1208:     version_statuses.each do |version, status|
1209:       version.update_attribute :status, status
1210:     end
1211: 
1212:     # Relations after in case issues related each other
1213:     project.issues.each do |issue|
1214:       new_issue = issues_map[issue.id]
1215:       unless new_issue
1216:         # Issue was not copied
1217:         next
1218:       end
1219: 
1220:       # Relations
1221:       issue.relations_from.each do |source_relation|
1222:         new_issue_relation = IssueRelation.new
1223:         new_issue_relation.attributes =
1224:           source_relation.attributes.dup.except("id", "issue_from_id", "issue_to_id")
1225:         new_issue_relation.issue_to = issues_map[source_relation.issue_to_id]
1226:         if new_issue_relation.issue_to.nil? && Setting.cross_project_issue_relations?
1227:           new_issue_relation.issue_to = source_relation.issue_to
1228:         end
1229:         new_issue.relations_from << new_issue_relation
1230:       end
1231: 
1232:       issue.relations_to.each do |source_relation|
1233:         new_issue_relation = IssueRelation.new
1234:         new_issue_relation.attributes =
1235:           source_relation.attributes.dup.except("id", "issue_from_id", "issue_to_id")
1236:         new_issue_relation.issue_from = issues_map[source_relation.issue_from_id]
1237:         if new_issue_relation.issue_from.nil? && Setting.cross_project_issue_relations?
1238:           new_issue_relation.issue_from = source_relation.issue_from
1239:         end
1240:         new_issue.relations_to << new_issue_relation
1241:       end
1242:     end
1243:   end

1245:   # Copies members from +project+
1246:   def copy_members(project)
1247:     # Copy users first, then groups to handle members with inherited and given roles
1248:     user_memberships, group_memberships =
1249:       project.memberships.partition {|m| m.principal.is_a?(User)}
1250:     members_to_copy = user_memberships + group_memberships
1251: 
1252:     members_to_copy.each do |member|
1253:       new_member = Member.new
1254:       new_member.attributes = member.attributes.dup.except("id", "project_id", "created_on")
1255:       # only copy non inherited roles
1256:       # inherited roles will be added when copying the group membership
1257:       role_ids = member.member_roles.reject(&:inherited?).collect(&:role_id)
1258:       next if role_ids.empty?
1259: 
1260:       new_member.role_ids = role_ids
1261:       new_member.project = self
1262:       self.members << new_member
1263:     end
1264:   end

1266:   # Copies queries from +project+
1267:   def copy_queries(project)
1268:     project.queries.each do |query|
1269:       new_query = query.class.new
1270:       new_query.attributes =
1271:         query.attributes.dup.except("id", "project_id", "sort_criteria",
1272:                                     "user_id", "type")
1273:       new_query.sort_criteria = query.sort_criteria if query.sort_criteria
1274:       new_query.project = self
1275:       new_query.user_id = query.user_id
1276:       new_query.role_ids = query.role_ids if query.visibility == ::Query::VISIBILITY_ROLES
1277:       self.queries << new_query
1278:       if query == project.default_issue_query
1279:         self.default_issue_query = new_query
1280:       end
1281:     end
1282:   end

1628:   # Query generator for selecting groups of issue counts for a project
1629:   # based on specific criteria
1630:   #
1631:   # Options
1632:   # * project - Project to search in.
1633:   # * with_subprojects - Includes subprojects issues if set to true.
1634:   # * association - Symbol. Association for grouping.
1635:   def self.count_and_group_by(options)
1636:     assoc = reflect_on_association(options[:association])
1637:     select_field = assoc.foreign_key
1638: 
1639:     Issue.
1640:       visible(User.current, :project => options[:project], :with_subprojects => options[:with_subprojects]).
1641:       joins(:status).
1642:       group(:status_id, :is_closed, select_field).
1643:       count.
1644:       map do |columns, total|
1645:         status_id, is_closed, field_value = columns
1646:         is_closed = ['t', 'true', '1'].include?(is_closed.to_s)
1647:         {
1648:           "status_id" => status_id.to_s,
1649:           "closed" => is_closed,
1650:           select_field => field_value.to_s,
1651:           "total" => total.to_s
1652:         }
1653:       end
1654:   end

1843:   # Updates the nested set for when an existing issue is moved
1844:   def update_nested_set_attributes_on_parent_change
1845:     former_parent_id = parent_id_before_last_save
1846:     # delete invalid relations of all descendants

1863:   def recalculate_attributes_for(issue_id)
1864:     if issue_id && p = Issue.find_by_id(issue_id)
1865:       if p.priority_derived?
1866:         # priority = highest priority of open children
1867:         # priority is left unchanged if all children are closed and there's no default priority defined
1868:         if priority_position =
1869:              p.children.open.joins(:priority).maximum("#{IssuePriority.table_name}.position")
1870:           p.priority = IssuePriority.find_by_position(priority_position)
1871:         elsif default_priority = IssuePriority.default
1872:           p.priority = default_priority
1873:         end
1874:       end
1875: 
1876:       if p.dates_derived?
1877:         # start/due dates = lowest/highest dates of children
1878:         p.start_date = p.children.minimum(:start_date)
1879:         p.due_date = p.children.maximum(:due_date)
1880:         if p.start_date && p.due_date && p.due_date < p.start_date
1881:           p.start_date, p.due_date = p.due_date, p.start_date
1882:         end
1883:       end
1884: 
1885:       if p.done_ratio_derived?
1886:         # done ratio = average ratio of children weighted with their total estimated hours
1887:         unless Issue.use_status_for_done_ratio? && p.status && p.status.default_done_ratio
1888:           children = p.children.to_a
1889:           if children.any?
1890:             child_with_total_estimated_hours = children.select {|c| c.total_estimated_hours.to_f > 0.0}
1891:             if child_with_total_estimated_hours.any?
1892:               average = Rational(
1893:                 child_with_total_estimated_hours.sum(&:total_estimated_hours).to_s,
1894:                 child_with_total_estimated_hours.count
1895:               )
1896:             else
1897:               average = Rational(1)
1898:             end
1899:             done = children.sum do |c|
1900:               estimated = Rational(c.total_estimated_hours.to_f.to_s)
1901:               estimated = average unless estimated > 0.0
1902:               ratio = c.closed? ? 100 : (c.done_ratio || 0)
1903:               estimated * ratio
1904:             end
1905:             progress = Rational(done, average * children.count)
1906:             p.done_ratio = progress.floor
1907:           end
1908:         end
1909:       end
1910: 
1911:       # ancestors will be recursively updated
1912:       p.save(:validate => false)
1913:     end
1914:   end

136:   # Returns a SQL conditions string used to find all issues visible by the specified user
137:   def self.visible_condition(user, options={})
138:     Project.allowed_to_condition(user, :view_issues, options) do |role, user|
139:       sql =
140:         if user.id && user.logged?
141:           case role.issues_visibility
142:           when 'all'
143:             '1=1'
144:           when 'default'
145:             user_ids = [user.id] + user.groups.pluck(:id).compact
146:             "(#{table_name}.is_private = #{connection.quoted_false} " \
147:               "OR #{table_name}.author_id = #{user.id} " \
148:               "OR #{table_name}.assigned_to_id IN (#{user_ids.join(',')}))"
149:           when 'own'
150:             user_ids = [user.id] + user.groups.pluck(:id).compact
151:             "(#{table_name}.author_id = #{user.id} OR " \
152:               "#{table_name}.assigned_to_id IN (#{user_ids.join(',')}))"
153:           else
154:             '1=0'
155:           end
156:         else
157:           "(#{table_name}.is_private = #{connection.quoted_false})"
158:         end
159:       unless role.permissions_all_trackers?(:view_issues)
160:         tracker_ids = role.permissions_tracker_ids(:view_issues)
161:         if tracker_ids.any?
162:           sql = "(#{sql} AND #{table_name}.tracker_id IN (#{tracker_ids.join(',')}))"
163:         else
164:           sql = '1=0'
165:         end
166:       end
167:       sql
168:     end
169:   end
170: 
171:   # Returns true if usr or current user is allowed to view the issue
172:   def visible?(usr=nil)
173:     (usr || User.current).allowed_to?(:view_issues, self.project) do |role, user|
174:       visible =
175:         if user.logged?
176:           case role.issues_visibility
177:           when 'all'
178:             true
179:           when 'default'
180:             !self.is_private? || (self.author == user || user.is_or_belongs_to?(assigned_to))
181:           when 'own'
182:             self.author == user || user.is_or_belongs_to?(assigned_to)
183:           else
184:             false
185:           end
186:         else
187:           !self.is_private?
188:         end
189:       unless role.permissions_all_trackers?(:view_issues)
190:         visible &&= role.permissions_tracker_ids?(:view_issues, tracker_id)
191:       end
192:       visible
193:     end
194:   end
195: 
196:   # Returns true if user or current user is allowed to log time on the issue
197:   def time_loggable?(user=User.current)
198:     user.allowed_to?(:log_time, project) && (Setting.timelog_accept_closed_issues? || !closed?)
199:   end
200: 
201:   # Returns true if user or current user is allowed to edit or add notes to the issue
202:   def editable?(user=User.current)
203:     attributes_editable?(user) || notes_addable?(user)
204:   end
205: 
206:   # Returns true if user or current user is allowed to edit the issue
207:   def attributes_editable?(user=User.current)
208:     user_tracker_permission?(user, :edit_issues) || (
209:       user_tracker_permission?(user, :edit_own_issues) && author == user
210:     )
211:   end
212: 
213:   def attachments_addable?(user=User.current)
214:     attributes_editable?(user) || notes_addable?(user)
215:   end
216: 
217:   # Overrides Redmine::Acts::Attachable::InstanceMethods#attachments_editable?
218:   def attachments_editable?(user=User.current)
219:     visible?(user) && attributes_editable?(user)
220:   end
221: 
222:   # Returns true if user or current user is allowed to add notes to the issue
223:   def notes_addable?(user=User.current)
224:     user_tracker_permission?(user, :add_issue_notes)
225:   end
226: 
227:   # Returns true if user or current user is allowed to delete the issue
228:   def deletable?(user=User.current)
229:     user_tracker_permission?(user, :delete_issues)
230:   end
231: 
232:   # Overrides Redmine::Acts::Attachable::InstanceMethods#attachments_deletable?
233:   def attachments_deletable?(user=User.current)
234:     visible?(user) && attributes_editable?(user)
235:   end
